# Knowns & Unknowns

The Understand loop's spine: one monotonic L-id space. Unknowns are attempted every round; only a
proven dry streak surfaces the human agenda.

## Unknowns

### L1: Will reviewers trust an AI-restated problem over their own brief?
kind: unknown
state: research-exhausted
load_bearing: true
assumption: true
attempts: 2
spawned_by: L0
ask: Do you, as a reviewer, act on the restated problem, or keep working from the original brief?
receipts: [[02 Research/Interviews|R1]] "The screenshot lied to me."
note: Desk research is exhausted; this needs a real reviewer in the room.

### L2: What is the smallest unit worth a receipt?
kind: unknown
state: open
attempts: 1
note: Still being attempted: a card, a clause, or a whole decision.

### L3: Which skipped steps most need to show honestly?
kind: unknown
state: research-exhausted
attempts: 2
spawned_by: L1
ask: Which omissions have burned you before, so we surface those first?

### L4: Is one signal enough to interrupt the team?
kind: unknown
state: open
attempts: 1

## Knowns

### L5: A production build starts deterministically enough to test against
kind: known
state: verified
attempts: 1
answered_by: L2
receipts: [[02 Research/Company|constraints]] "Single-user, local, dev-oriented."
note: Confirmed against the Next.js production server; a stable smoke target.

### L7: Reviewers read a real measure without zooming
kind: known
state: unverified
load_bearing: true
assumption: true
attempts: 1
note: Lightly evidenced from typography research; treat as an assumption until observed.
